source_jobs.title: Engineering Lead- Options
source_jobs.description:
Engineering Lead: Options



About Keyrock



Since our beginnings in 2017, we've grown to be a leading change-maker in the digital asset space, renowned for our partnerships and innovation.



Today, we rock with over 200 team members around the world. Our diverse team hails from 42 nationalities, with backgrounds ranging from DeFi natives to PhDs. Predominantly remote, we have hubs in London, Madrid, Brussels, Singapore , New York and Paris, and host regular online and offline hangouts to keep the crew tight.



We are trading on more than 80 exchanges, and working with a wide array of asset issuers. As a well-established market maker, our distinctive expertise led us to expand rapidly. Today, our services span market making, options trading, high-frequency trading, OTC, and DeFi trading desks as well as asset and wealth management. Keyrock is looking to expand and establish itself as a full-service capital markets and asset & wealth management financial institution through both organic innovation and inorganic growth.



But we’re more than a service provider. We’re an initiator. We have been pioneers in adopting the Rust Development language for our algorithmic trading systems, and champions of its use in the industry. We upgrade Web3 ecosystems by injecting liquidity into promising DeFi, RWA, and NFT protocols. And we push the industry's progress with our research and governance initiatives.



At Keyrock, we're not just envisioning the future of digital assets. We're actively building it.





Mission statement
We are looking for a Head of Engineering / Engineering Lead with strong experience in systematic options trading to lead our Options Engineering team.

In this role, you will be responsible for leading the Options Engineering team, driving continuous improvement across the trading infrastructure, and supporting the evolution of our systematic options trading platform. This includes areas such as automated quoting, execution, risk controls, pricing workflows, and production reliability.

We are primarily looking for candidates based in European time zones or the US East Coast time zone.

We are looking for someone who can:

 - Lead and grow an Options Engineering team of around 7 engineers

 - Bring hands-on experience in systematic options trading, ideally including options market-making, automated quoting, execution, and risk management

 - Own and evolve an existing Rust-native systematic options trading platform

 - Provide strong technical direction across trading infrastructure, system design, performance, reliability, and risk controls

 - Work pragmatically through complex engineering and trading-system challenges

 - Partner closely with trading, quant, product, risk, and infrastructure stakeholders

 - Balance technical leadership with people leadership, delivery ownership, and operational discipline



Required Experience

 - 7+ years of experience in systematic trading, options market-making or low-latency trading systems

 - Strong hands-on experience with Rust, C++, or C

 - Willingness to become deeply proficient in Rust, if not already

 - Strong understanding of algorithmic options trading, including automated quoting, execution, pricing, risk controls, and market data workflows

 - Experience building or maintaining low-latency trading systems, ideally within options or derivatives markets

 - Experience working with SQL databases in production environments, with PostgreSQL preferred

 - Familiarity with functional testing, component testing, and production-grade validation practices

 - Strong understanding of distributed systems, service boundaries, and production reliability

 - Strong knowledge of gRPC and experience working with Node.js-based services is preferred

 - Previous experience with crypto exchange connectivity and/or FPGA technologies is strongly preferred



Leadership & Working Style

 - Strong team player with the ability to lead, mentor, and guide engineers

 - Pragmatic approa

source_jobs.title: Director, Corporate Sales US - Digital Assets
source_jobs.description:
link

About Keyrock



Since our beginnings in 2017, we've grown to be a leading change-maker in the digital asset space, renowned for our partnerships and innovation.



Today, we rock with over 200 team members around the world. Our diverse team hails from 42 nationalities, with backgrounds ranging from DeFi natives to PhDs. Predominantly remote, we have hubs in London, Brussels, Singapore and Paris, and host regular online and offline hangouts to keep the crew tight.



We are trading on more than 80 exchanges, and working with a wide array of asset issuers. As a well-established market maker, our distinctive expertise led us to expand rapidly. Today, our services span market making, options trading, high-frequency trading, OTC, and DeFi trading desks as well as digital asset management. Keyrock is looking to expand and establish itself as a full-service financial institution through both organic innovation and inorganic growth.



But we’re more than a service provider. We’re an initiator. We're pioneers in adopting the Rust Development language for our algorithmic trading systems, and champions of its use in the industry. We support the growth of Web3 startups through our Accelerator Program. We upgrade ecosystems by injecting liquidity into promising DeFi, RWA, and NFT protocols. And we push the industry's progress with our research and governance initiatives.



At Keyrock, we're not just envisioning the future of digital assets. We're actively building it.



Mission statement



Build and scale Keyrock’s US commercial presence by originating, negotiating, and closing high-impact partnerships with foundations, token issuers, ecosystem treasuries, and institutional digital-asset players.

You’ll drive the expansion of Keyrock’s US client base, position our liquidity, derivatives, and structured solutions, and help define the commercial strategy for the largest market in crypto. This will be done in close collaboration with the Head of Corporate Sales and the Chief Commercial Officer, ensuring alignment between global capabilities and US-market needs.



Job description



Business Development & Market Expansion

 - Build and grow Keyrock’s US presence with a strong focus on foundations, token issuers, L1s/L2s, and ecosystem treasuries.

 - Originate and qualify high-value opportunities across the entire token lifecycle: pre-TGE advisory, launch market making, OTC solutions, and post-listing liquidity programs.

 - Lead client conversations with founders, investors, core teams, and boards — positioning Keyrock’s liquidity, derivatives, and product capabilities.
   

Commercial Execution & Structuring Interface

 - Translate client needs into actionable commercial proposals that leverage Keyrock’s full offering:
   market making, derivatives, structured products, treasury management, tokenomic advisory.

 - Work closely with Structuring, Trading, Risk, and Product to ensure commercial proposals are feasible, underwritten correctly, and aligned with risk frameworks.

 - Support pricing, modeling inputs, and scenario framing for US-driven opportunities.
   

US Franchise Building

 - Work hand-in-hand with the Head of Corporate Sales and the CCO to develop Keyrock’s US commercial roadmap, priorities, and client segmentation.

 - Shape and implement US go-to-market processes, CRM discipline, and reporting structures.

 - Represent Keyrock at US conferences, industry events, and protocol-specific gatherings to expand visibility and pipeline.
   

Internal Evangelization & Stakeholder Alignment

 - Communicate US market trends, ecosystem signals, and competitive intelligence to global product, trading, and commercial teams.

 - Act together with the Head of Corporate Sales as the primary commercial US liaison for internal stakeholders: Legal, Risk, Compliance, Finance, and Product — ensuring alignment and shortening review cycles.

 - Support internal adoption of best practices, documentation standards, and commercial template

source_jobs.title: Senior Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
SENIOR DATA ENGINEER




ABOUT KEYROCK

Since our beginnings in 2017, we've grown to be a leading change-maker in the digital asset space, renowned for our partnerships and innovation.

Today, we partner with over 250 team members around the world. Our diverse team hails from 42 nationalities, with backgrounds ranging from self-taught DeFi natives to PhDs. Predominantly remote, we have hubs in London, Brussels, and Singapore, and host regular online and offline hangouts to keep the crew tight.

We are trading on more than 80 trading venues, and working with a wide array of asset issuers. As a well-established market maker, our distinctive expertise led us to expand rapidly. Today, our services span market making, options trading, high-frequency trading, OTC, and DeFi trading desks.

But we’re more than a service provider. We’re an initiator. We're pioneers in adopting the Rust Development language for our algorithmic trading, and champions of its use in the industry. We support the growth of Web3 startups through our Accelerator Program. We upgrade ecosystems by injecting liquidity into promising DeFi, RWA, and NFT protocols. And we push the industry's progress with our research and governance initiatives.

At Keyrock, we're not just envisioning the future of digital assets. We're actively building it.




ABOUT THE TEAM AND WHY THE ROLE EXISTS

The Central Data Team (CDT) is only a few months old, but data has been Keyrock's lifeblood since day one. We're now building the Keyrock Data Platform to give Keyrockers, and the AI agents working alongside them, the data and context they need to act fast and autonomously within agreed boundaries and aligned with our shared goals. Doing that means taking data from across the company and making sense of it in real time for all the functions that depend on it: trading desks, wealth and asset management, product, risk, finance, compliance, and research to name a few.

You'd be one of the early hires in CDT and we expect you to contribute to most of the bigger decisions and build work. The standards we settle on and the way the team ends up working are still open.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build streaming and batch pipelines that ingest, normalise, and distribute market, trading, and portfolio data, resilient to feed and exchange failures.

 - Build the self-serve tooling (SDKs, patterns, templates, AI agents) so other teams publish, consume, and build on data products without waiting on us.

 - Own data contracts and schema evolution. Keep schema changes from turning into multi-team coordination events.

 - Design the lakehouse and time-series layer around consumer query patterns.

 - Build and evolve the Data Governance and Data Quality Framework: stale-feed detection, schema validation, range checks, idempotent writes, lineage, ownership, self-healing.

 - Build the derived analytics the business runs on: cross-exchange spreads, VWAP at depth, order book microstructure for the desks; portfolio views, exposure, performance for wealth and asset management.

 - Make observability, cost, and performance first-class from day one.

 - Treat infrastructure as code (Docker, Terraform, CI/CD) alongside our Central Infrastructure Team.

 - Work in the open: write things down, partner closely with Architecture, Infrastructure, Platform, and the rest of the teams.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Tools matter less to us than how you think about problems. That said, here's the shape of the person we have in mind.

Engineering Craft

 - 8+ years of building production data systems that other people rely on.

 - Strong proficiency in Python and SQL: not just being able to write a query, but being able to reason about what the engine is doing with it.

 - Code that's easy for someone else to read, test, and delete later.

 - Strong understanding of data modelling for both streaming and analytical workloads.

 - Efficiency, quality, idempotency, and observability are taken seriously by default.

Systems Design

source_jobs.title: Executive Director- Corporate Sales
source_jobs.description:
Keyrock is expanding its global commercial presence across digital asset markets and is looking to hire an Executive Director, Corporate Sales to act as a deputy to the Managing Director, Global Corporate Sales.

This is a high-impact role combining strategic deal origination with ownership of key institutional relationships across the crypto ecosystem. We seek an individual with ambition and skills to take up a global leadership role in time.

Working closely with trading teams, you will develop partnerships with token issuers, foundations and institutional market participants, bringing Keyrock’s market-making, derivatives and liquidity capabilities to market. Alongside driving revenue and strategic accounts, you will act as a close commercial partner to the MD of Global Corporate Sales, with scope to evolve toward broader global commercial responsibilities as the business continues to scale.

If you have a strong track record building institutional relationships in crypto or financial markets and enjoy operating at the intersection of markets, technology, and strategy, we would like to hear from you.

About Keyrock

Founded in 2017, Keyrock is a leading digital asset market maker with more than 200 team members globally across 40+ nationalities. We trade on 80+ exchanges and operate desks spanning market making, options, OTC, and DeFi. Known for our technology-first approach and Rust-based infrastructure, we continue to play an active role in shaping the structure of digital asset markets.

Key Delivery Areas

Strategic Deal Origination

 - Identify and engage high-value corporate counterparties including token issuers, blockchain foundations, funds, exchanges and institutional market participants.

 - Build and maintain a structured pipeline of opportunities across derivatives, liquidity provision and market-making partnerships.

 - Lead complex commercial discussions from initial engagement through structuring and closing.

 - Strategic Account Ownership

 - Act as the primary relationship owner for a portfolio of key corporate clients.

 - Develop long-term partnerships by understanding client objectives and aligning them with tailored trading and liquidity solutions.

 - Maintain ongoing dialogue with clients to anticipate needs, identify expansion opportunities and ensure strong client satisfaction.

Strategic Expansion of Client Services

 - Collaborate closely with trading, research and product teams to design bespoke solutions for corporate clients.

 - Identify opportunities to expand existing relationships through derivatives offerings, liquidity programs and customised market-making models.

 - Contribute to the development of new commercial initiatives aligned with evolving market demand.

Market Intelligence & Fundamental Analysis

 - Conduct fundamental analysis on blockchain projects, token economics and emerging ecosystems to evaluate partnership opportunities.

 - Leverage financial, on-chain and market data to assess the strategic relevance of potential counterparties.

 - Provide insights and recommendations that inform commercial strategy and partnership decisions.

 - Commercial Leadership

 - Act as a strategic partner to the MD of GlobalCorporate Sales in shaping go-to-market priorities, managing strategic accounts and developing the corporate sales function.

 - Support coordination across the sales organization by mentoring junior team members, sharing best practices,and helping structure complex deals.

 - Represent the corporate sales function in cross-functional initiatives and selected external engagements.

Background & Experience

 - 8+ years of experience in institutional sales, business development or strategic partnerships within financial services or digital asset markets.

 - Strong understanding of derivatives, trading concepts, and market structure, ideally gained through direct exposure to trading desks or derivatives products.

 - Deep familiarity with digital asset markets, including
